images.caption: Figure 1:SWE-Bench accuracy vs. (a) cumulative rollout turns and (b) cumulative rollout time during training, using the same number of compute nodes. PivotRL reaches comparable accuracy to E2E RL with∼4×{\sim}4\timesfewer rollout turns and∼5.5×{\sim}5.5\timesless wall-clock time.
Figure 2:τ2\tau^{2}-Bench accuracy over RL training.𝒟a​d​v\mathcal{D}_{adv}yields the strongest optimization and outperform same-data SFT.
Figure 2:τ2\tau^{2}-Bench accuracy over RL training.𝒟a​d​v\mathcal{D}_{adv}yields the strongest optimization and outperform same-data SFT.
Figure 3:τ2\tau^{2}-Bench reward standard deviation during RL training.𝒟a​d​v\mathcal{D}_{adv}sets maintain higher reward variance and therefore more informative local updates.
